frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Arre, I always say, simple and natural is best. Aajkal some women put so much makeup, then in real life they look completely different — that is also a kind of misleading only. But haan, basic grooming toh hona chahiye, otherwise it looks like she has stopped taking care of herself.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: criticizing visible makeup as deceptive while also expecting women to maintain appearance through 'basic grooming.' It encodes the surface manifestations of 'natural beauty' policing, deception framing, and appearance obligation.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
